perturbation.original_text: 1.16 "Problem" means a Severity Level 1, 2, 3 or 4 problem with a Supported Product (whether or not attributable or believed to be attributable to an Error), as such problems are described in greater detail in Exhibit A hereto.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.16 "Problem" means a Severity Level 1 or 2 problem with a Supported Product (whether or not attributable or believed to be attributable to an Error), as such problems are described in greater detail in Exhibit A hereto. For the purposes of this agreement, severity levels 3 and 4, while technically problems, will not be considered problems for VNE's obligation to provide support.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by limiting the definition of 'Problem' to only Severity Levels 1 and 2, despite Exhibit A describing Severity Levels 1, 2, 3, and 4. This is contradictory because, later in the contract, VNE's responsibilities are defined by all 4 severity levels. This creates ambiguity about which issues VNE must address. In section 2.1, It's contradictory due to conflicting implications as Exhibit A outlines all severity levels and now we made a change that only severity levels 1 and 2 will be addressed, creating uncertainty about the obligations of each party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.1 General. The Maintenance and Support Services provided by VNE hereunder shall encompass responding to Problem Reports and Support Requests, and providing Converge with Maintenance Updates, as more particularly described in, and subject to the provisions of, this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text narrows the definition of 'Problem' to only Severity Levels 1 and 2, while the contradicted text (2.1) refers to Maintenance and Support Services responding to all 'Problem Reports'. Since the original Exhibit A (mentioned in both sections) defines Severity Levels 1, 2, 3, and 4, the changed text creates a direct conflict by limiting the scope of problems VNE is obligated to address. This is a structural flaw leading to in-text contradiction because the definition of 'Problem' has conflicting implications.

perturbation.original_text: 4.3 Application of Quarterly Allocation; Out-Of-Scope Services. For each quarter during the Term of this Agreement that Converge pays the applicable Monthly Installments, Converge shall be entitled to receive Services in an amount equal to the Quarterly Allocation; provided, however, that VNE shall provide all Maintenance and Support Services with respect to Problem Reports (but not with respect to Support Requests) reported during such quarter, and all Maintenance Updates released by VNE during such quarter, regardless of whether the provision of such Services would cause VNE to exceed the Quarterly Allocation for such quarter; and provided further, however, that if Converge fails to make a Monthly Installment when due (subject to the cure period in Section 9.2(c)), VNE shall not be required to provided additional Services during the remaining portion of the applicable quarter until such payment is made.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.3 Application of Quarterly Allocation; Out-Of-Scope Services. For each quarter during the Term of this Agreement that Converge pays the applicable Monthly Installments, Converge shall be entitled to receive Services in an amount equal to the Quarterly Allocation; provided, however, that VNE shall provide Maintenance and Support Services with respect to Problem Reports (but not with respect to Support Requests) reported during such quarter, and Maintenance Updates released by VNE during such quarter, only if the provision of such Services would not cause VNE to exceed the Quarterly Allocation for such quarter; and provided further, however, that if Converge fails to make a Monthly Installment when due (subject to the cure period in Section 9.2(c)), VNE shall not be required to provided additional Services during the remaining portion of the applicable quarter until such payment is made.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction to the contract. The original text stated that VNE would provide all maintenance and support regardless of the Quarterly Allocation. However, the updated version stipulates that VNE will only provide services if it does not exceed the Quarterly Allocation, thus creating an in-text contradiction in where VNE's responsibilities are defined by all 4 severity levels. This change is contradictory because, this creates ambiguity about which issues VNE must address, and will cause to impact any of the supported products. In section 2.1, it creates uncertainty about the obligations of each party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.1 General. The Maintenance and Support Services provided by VNE hereunder shall encompass responding to Problem Reports and Support Requests, and providing Converge with Maintenance Updates, as more particularly described in, and subject to the provisions of, this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 4.3 stated that VNE would provide all Maintenance and Support Services regardless of the Quarterly Allocation. The changed text in Section 4.3 modifies this by stating that VNE will only provide these services if it does not exceed the Quarterly Allocation. This directly contradicts the original obligation to provide all maintenance and support, creating an in-text contradiction with Section 2.1, which generally describes VNE's obligations to provide Maintenance and Support Services. The change introduces a clear conflict regarding the scope of VNE's responsibilities, causing uncertainty and potential unenforceability. Therefore, based on the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction', the answer is YES.

perturbation.original_text: 5.1 Services Warranty. VNE warrants that the Services provided hereunder will be provided in accordance with generally-accepted industry standards applicable to the performance of services of a similar nature. In the event of any breach of the foregoing warranty, and provided that Converge reports such breach to VNE in writing within 90 days following the date of performance of the Services in question, VNE shall, as its sole obligation and Converge's sole and exclusive remedy, promptly repair, replace or re-perform the Services in question, without additional cost to Converge, so as to correct the warranty non-compliance as promptly as practicable (within 30 days to the extent technically feasible).
perturbation.changed_text: 5.1 Services Warranty. VNE warrants that the Services provided hereunder will be provided in accordance with generally-accepted industry standards applicable to the performance of services of a similar nature. In the event of any breach of the foregoing warranty, and provided that Converge reports such breach to VNE in writing within 90 days following the date of performance of the Services in question, VNE may, at its sole discretion, repair, replace, or re-perform the Services in question, without additional cost to Converge, so as to correct the warranty non-compliance as promptly as practicable (within 30 days to the extent technically feasible).
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces uncertainty by modifying the warranty clause. The original text stated that if the warranty is breached VNE 'shall' promptly repair, replace, or re-perform the services in question. This has been changed to VNE 'may, at its sole discretion' perform these tasks. This directly contradicts the nature of a warranty, which implies an obligation, not a choice. This could affect section 6.1 by making it unclear what damages are covered due to VNE now having the option not to fulfill the original contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.1 Disclaimer of Liability for Certain Damages. 6.1.1 Consequential and Similar Damages. TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY LAW, IN NO EVENT SHALL EITHER PARTY BE LIABLE OR OBLIGATED IN ANY MANNER FOR ANY SPECIAL, INCIDENTAL, EXEMPLARY OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES OF ANY KIND ARISING OUT OF OR RELATING TO THIS AGREEMENT (INCLUDING, BUT NOT LIMITED TO, LOST PROFITS, REVENUES OR BUSINESS OPPORTUNITIES) HOWEVER CAUSED AND REGARDLESS OF THE FORM OF ACTION, WHETHER IN CONTRACT, TORT, NEGLIGENCE, STRICT PRODUCT LIABILITY, OR OTHERWISE, EVEN IF THE PARTY HAS BEEN INFORMED OF THE POSSIBILITY OF ANY SUCH DAMAGES IN ADVANCE. The foregoing limitation 12 is independent of any exclusive remedies available to either party under this Agreement, including any failure of such remedies.
perturbation.justification: The original text in section 5.1 stated VNE 'shall' repair, replace, or re-perform services if the warranty is breached, creating a clear obligation. The changed text modifies this to VNE 'may, at its sole discretion' perform these tasks. This removes the obligation, introducing uncertainty. This directly contradicts section 6.1, which assumes that there are exclusive remedies available under the Agreement and anticipates potential 'failure of such remedies'. By making the remedy optional, section 5.1 undermines the premise of section 6.1, creating a strong contradiction.
